key_insight: XCT-SAM improves industrial XCT defect segmentation by adapting SAM in two stages, first through alloy microstructures and then to XCT, which helps bridge the large domain gap.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical low-parameter way to deploy foundation models for additive manufacturing quality control where labeled XCT data are scarce and defects are hard to segment.
method: Fine-tune SAM with Conv-LoRA adapters instead of full-model training.
Use sequential domain adaptation: natural image prior -> alloy microstructure -> XCT.
Train only about 4.15M parameters while keeping over 99% of SAM frozen.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on out-of-distribution CycleGAN-XCT benchmarks and real NIST XCT scans, where it achieved the best overall IoU and Dice scores versus zero-shot SAM and other adapted baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the intermediate alloy-microstructure transfer and on how well the adapted model generalizes to new XCT scan conditions.
paper_title: XCT-SAM: Sequential Parameter-Efficient Domain Adaptation of SAM for Industrial XCT Defect Segmentation
